model_metadata.business_use_case: QSR audience targeting and campaign optimization
model_metadata.decision_supported: prioritize, suppress, or recalibrate synthetic QSR audience activation

features.name: qsr_txn_count_30d
features.business_definition: Number of synthetic QSR transactions in the last 30 days.

features.name: qsr_spend_30d
features.business_definition: Total synthetic QSR spend in the last 30 days.

features.name: qsr_recency_days
features.business_definition: Days since the synthetic consumer's most recent QSR transaction.

features.name: competitor_qsr_share_90d
features.business_definition: Share of synthetic QSR activity with competitor QSR merchants over 90 days.

features.name: grocery_spend_30d
features.business_definition: Total synthetic grocery spend in the last 30 days.

features.name: fuel_spend_30d
features.business_definition: Total synthetic fuel spend in the last 30 days.

features.name: weekend_dining_frequency
features.business_definition: Count of synthetic weekend dining events in the last 30 days.

features.name: merchant_novelty_rate
features.business_definition: Rate of synthetic dining activity at merchants not previously observed for the consumer.

features.name: campaign_exposed
features.business_definition: Indicator that the synthetic consumer was exposed to a QSR campaign.

rule_hierarchy: High if any high-severity data-quality issue exists.
High if two or more high-drift features exist.
High if max absolute cluster share shift is at or above 10.00 percentage points.
High if prediction drift level is High.
Medium if any medium-severity data-quality issue exists.
Medium if one high-drift feature exists.
Medium if two or more medium-drift features exist.
Medium if max absolute cluster share shift is at or above 5.00 percentage points.
Medium if prediction drift level is Medium.
Low if none of the configured High or Medium rules fire.
